scripture.text: Be still, and know that I am God.

today.reflection: The command before the knowing is stillness. Not analysis, not strategy, not more effort — stillness. In a week that has asked a lot of you, pause long enough to remember that the One who holds everything doesn't flinch at what you're holding. Your quiet is not wasted. It is where knowing begins.
today.question: What would stillness look like in the next hour?

archive.preview: They that wait on the Lord shall renew their strength.
The Lord is my shepherd — the present tense matters.
All things. Not some things. Not the pretty things.
Come to me, all who are weary — that's an invitation, not a test.
Anxious for nothing means there is room for everything to be prayed.
For God alone my soul waits in silence.
Peace I leave with you — His peace, not the world's.
New every morning. Not recycled. Not second-hand.
Trust in the Lord with all your heart — not a slice of it.
My grace is sufficient. Not barely — sufficient.
Fearfully and wonderfully made — including today.
Be strong and courageous — because He goes with you.
To whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life.
Close to the broken-hearted. Not cautiously near — close.
Draw near with confidence — you are welcome at the throne.
Be transformed by the renewing of your mind.
A season for everything — including the one you're in.
Seek first the kingdom — the rest gets added.
In Your presence is fullness of joy.
You have searched me and known me.
If any of you lacks wisdom, ask.
I have called you by name. You are mine.
Cast all your anxiety on Him, for He cares for you.
He who dwells in the shelter of the Most High.
The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace…
Nothing can separate us from the love of God.
An anchor for the soul, firm and secure.
Wait for the Lord. Be strong. Take heart.
The heart plans its way; the Lord directs the steps.
I am with you always, to the end of the age.
